CheckTitle: Ensure named locations are defined for Conditional Access
Description: Checks whether at least one named location (IP-based or country-based) is configured. Named locations are essential for geo-fencing, blocking sign-ins from untrusted regions, and defining trusted network perimeters in Conditional Access policies.
Risk: Without named locations, Conditional Access policies cannot enforce location-based controls — no trusted IP ranges, no country-level blocking, and no impossible-travel detection context. This removes a key defense layer against foreign nation-state actors.

Remediation.Recommendation: Define named locations for your trusted IP ranges (corporate networks, VPNs) and permitted countries. Use these in Conditional Access policies to block or restrict sign-ins from unknown locations.

Frameworks.source: AI-generated mapping — not yet verified against published benchmark
